---
title: "Division Visualizations (306)"
author: "Alexis Golart"
date: "2026-03-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 7
)
```

```{r}

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(stringr)
library(viridis)
```

### Individual Division Visuals 
```{r}
file_path <- "/Users/alexi/Downloads/308_companies_by_division.xlsx"
sheet_names <- excel_sheets(file_path)

for (sheet in sheet_names) {
  
  # Read one division
  df <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = sheet)
  
  # Clean names a little for prettier titles
  nice_names <- names(df)
  nice_names[-1] <- str_to_title(nice_names[-1])
  names(df) <- nice_names
  
  # Long format
  df_long <- df %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -Year,
      names_to = "Word",
      values_to = "Count"
    )
  
  # unique words/categories
  words <- unique(df_long$Word)
  
  # choose a nice palette
  pal <- viridis(length(words), option = "D", end = 0.9)
  
  # make one graph per word
  plot_list <- vector("list", length(words))
  
  for (i in seq_along(words)) {
    w <- words[i]
    
    p <- df_long %>%
      filter(Word == w) %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = Count)) +
      geom_area(fill = pal[i], alpha = 0.25) +
      geom_line(color = pal[i], linewidth = 1.1) +
      geom_point(color = pal[i], size = 1.8) +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = pretty_breaks(n = 6)) +
      labs(
        title = w,
        x = NULL,
        y = "Count"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)
      )
    
    plot_list[[i]] <- p
  }
  
  # Title for the page
  page_title <- ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = sheet,
             size = 7, fontface = "bold", hjust = 0.5) +
    theme_void()
  
  # combine onto one page
  combined_plot <- wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 2) +
    plot_annotation(title = sheet) &
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
  
  print(combined_plot)
  
}
```



### Grouped Industry Visuals 
```{r}
division_data <- lapply(sheet_names, function(sheet) {
  read_excel(file_path, sheet = sheet) %>%
    mutate(Division = sheet)
})

# combine all sheets
all_divisions <- bind_rows(division_data)

# reshape to long format
plot_data <- all_divisions %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(Year, Division),
    names_to = "Word",
    values_to = "Count"
  )

# get unique words
word_list <- unique(plot_data$Word)

# make one graph per word
for (w in word_list) {
  p <- plot_data %>%
    filter(Word == w) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = Count, color = Division)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Counts of", w, "Across Divisions Over Time"),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Count",
      color = "Division"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()

  print(p)
  cat('<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>')
  
}
```
